entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: An important food crop grown in arid and semi-arid tropical regions of Africa and Asia, valued for nutrition and climate resilience

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A continent where pearl millet is grown, specifically in West and Central regions

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: A continent where pearl millet is grown, specifically in India

entities.name: MALNUTRITION
entities.description: A health condition prevalent in poor smallholder households in arid and semi-arid regions

entities.name: FOOD AND NUTRITIONAL SECURITY
entities.description: The goal of ensuring access to sufficient and nutritious food for poor households

entities.name: CARBOHYDRATES
entities.description: A dietary component provided by pearl millet

entities.name: ENERGY
entities.description: A dietary component provided by pearl millet

entities.name: PROTEIN
entities.description: A dietary component provided by pearl millet

entities.name: CALCIUM
entities.description: A mineral provided by pearl millet

entities.name: IRON
entities.description: A mineral provided by pearl millet, deficiency of which causes anemia

entities.name: ZINC
entities.description: A mineral provided by pearl millet, deficiency of which causes stunting

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: A climate stress that pearl millet is resilient to

entities.name: HEAT
entities.description: A climate stress that pearl millet is resilient to

entities.name: STOVER
entities.description: The vegetative part of the pearl millet plant used as livestock fodder

entities.name: LIVESTOCK
entities.description: Animals that utilize pearl millet stover as a source of fodder

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A country in Asia growing biofortified pearl millet

entities.name: WEST AND CENTRAL AFRICA
entities.description: Regions in Africa where pearl millet is grown and tested

entities.name: ANEMIA
entities.description: A health problem caused by iron deficiency

entities.name: STUNTING
entities.description: A health problem caused by zinc deficiency

entities.name: BIOFORTIFICATION
entities.description: A cost-effective and sustainable agricultural strategy to increase nutrient densities in crops

entities.name: GENETIC VARIABILITY
entities.description: The natural range of iron and zinc levels available within pearl millet for breeding

entities.name: HIGH-YIELD
entities.description: A desired agricultural trait for pearl millet cultivars

entities.name: OPEN-POLLINATED VARIETIES
entities.description: A type of crop variety breeding, exemplified by Dhanshakti and Chakti

entities.name: DHANSHAKTI
entities.description: A biofortified open-pollinated variety of pearl millet

entities.name: CHAKTI
entities.description: A biofortified open-pollinated variety of pearl millet

entities.name: HYBRIDS
entities.description: A type of crop breeding producing high-yield cultivars like the ICMH series

entities.name: ICMH 1202
entities.description: A biofortified hybrid variety of pearl millet

entities.name: ICMH 1203
entities.description: A biofortified hybrid variety of pearl millet

entities.name: ICMH 1301
entities.description: A biofortified hybrid variety of pearl millet

entities.name: GENOMIC TOOLS
entities.description: Tools used in breeding programs for nutritional traits

entities.name: DIAGNOSTIC MARKERS
entities.description: Markers used within genomic tools for breeding nutritional traits

entities.name: GENOMIC SELECTION
entities.description: A method used within genomic tools for breeding nutritional traits

entities.name: CLINICAL STUDIES
entities.description: Research used to determine the bioavailability of nutrients in biofortified cultivars

entities.name: RECOMMENDED DAILY ALLOWANCE
entities.description: The standard daily intake of nutrients required for health

entities.name: POULTRY
entities.description: Animals that benefit from pearl millet as feed in non-traditional regions
